title: salpichlaena denticulata
intro: Salpichlaena denticulata, also known as the 'dental-leaved salpichlaena', is a rare and endemic fern species native to the Pacific island of New Guinea. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Salpichlaena denticulata include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
